articles.title: Trust Trump? Iran’s Doubts Shadow Peace Talks.
articles.summary: Iranian leaders fear being burned again by President Trump, who tore up a nuclear agreement reached during the Obama administration after lengthy negotiations.

articles.title: Kevin Warsh, Trump’s Fed Chair Pick, Faces Skepticism Over Claim of Independence
articles.summary: Kevin Warsh’s nomination to lead the Federal Reserve could still be delayed by a Justice Department investigation into the current chair, Jerome H. Powell.

articles.title: Trump Is Said to Be in Talks to Send Afghans Who Aided U.S. Forces to Congo
articles.summary: A U.S. aid worker said that the Afghans, who were evacuated to Qatar, would face a choice between moving to the Democratic Republic of Congo and living under the Taliban.

articles.title: Virginia Redistricting Referendum: What to Watch
articles.summary: Republicans have built a small advantage from the gerrymandering clash so far, but Virginia voters could change that.

articles.title: Instead of civil war, a naked mole rat colony changed queens peacefully
articles.summary: These matriarchal rodents often have  bloody succession wars to replace their queen. But in a colony in California, Queen Tere ceded the throne to her daughter, Arwen, without violence.

articles.title: As Pakistan seeks to broker U.S.–Iran peace, citizens watch for gains at home
articles.summary: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.

articles.title: What would happen next if Iran agreed to surrender its highly enriched uranium
articles.summary: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.

articles.title: U.S. Turns Up Pressure on Iraq to Distance Itself From Iran
articles.summary: Washington is demanding that the Baghdad government dismantle Iran-backed Iraqi militias that have been attacking Americans and U.S. sites there recently.

articles.title: Florida AG launches criminal investigation into ChatGPT over FSU shooting
articles.summary: Florida's attorney general is launching a criminal investigation into the alleged role of ChatGPT in a mass shooting at Florida State University last year.

articles.title: Florida Democrat Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick, facing possible expulsion, resigns
articles.summary: The Florida Democrat faced a potential expulsion vote in the House as she prepares for a federal trial on allegations that she stole disaster funds and used some of the money to finance her campaign.

articles.title: Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick, a Florida Democrat, Resigns From Congress
articles.summary: Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick stands accused of stealing $5 million in federal funds. She announced she would step down minutes before the House Ethics Committee was to vote on a recommendation to expel

articles.title: Southern Poverty Law Center Says It Is Under Investigation by Justice Dept.
articles.summary: The Southern Poverty Law Center, which is best known for investigating hate groups, has been accused by Republicans of unfairly targeting conservative and Christian organizations.

articles.title: Europe Wanted a Say on the Iran War, but It’s Still on the Sidelines
articles.summary: A British-French plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz would give the continent a role. But Tehran and Washington are still calling the shots.

articles.title: Want to lighten your mental load? First, let go of these gender myths
articles.summary: "Men can't see the mess." "Women are better at chores." These myths position women to take on more emotional thinking, says researcher Leah Ruppanner. She shares what works to reclaim your headspace.

articles.title: Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request
articles.summary: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.

articles.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire nears its end. And, Fed chair nominee faces tough hearing
articles.summary: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing

articles.title: Ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon
articles.summary: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.

articles.title: The hidden power keeping wages low
articles.summary: For decades, economists gave short shrift to the idea of monopsony — a power employers can have to suppress wages. Now a wave of research suggests it's everywhere, and a new book argues it's key to un

articles.title: Japan approves removing ban on lethal weapons exports
articles.summary: Japan endorsed scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports, a significant change of its postwar pacifist policy, as the country seeks to build up its arms industry.

articles.title: Gulf aluminum shipments stuck as Strait of Hormuz blockades put a strain on supply
articles.summary: A U.S. blockade and conflict in the Strait of Hormuz have stranded Gulf aluminum shipments and driven prices to a four-year high, exposing global supply vulnerabilities and deepening trade strain.

articles.title: Gunman shoots several tourists at historic pyramids in Mexico
articles.summary: A man standing atop one of the historic Teotihuacan pyramids opened fire on tourists Monday, killing one Canadian and leaving at least 13 people, authorities said.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
